owned and operated by Peel Ports. Road access is via Strand Road Gate <L20 1AF>.

Free Text: HIMALAYAN BALSAM (IMPATIENS GLANDULIFERA) INVASIVE WEED SPECIES : IS GROWING ON BOTH SIDES OF THE TRACK , AROUND THE TRAIN STATION AND NEARBY SUB STATION. SPREAD OVER SEVERAL 100 YARDS BOTH SIDES OF THE STATION PLATFORMS. IT IS CLASSED AS AN INVASIVE SPECIES WEED AND CARE NEEDS TO BE TAKEN DURING THE FLOWERING AND SEEDING TIMES AS IT CAN BE SPREAD BY INDIVIDUALS PASSING ON THE SEEDS. EACH PLANT CAN PRODUCE UP TO 800 SEEDS. THESE ARE DISPERSED WIDELY AS THE RIPE SEEDPODS SHOOT THEIR SEEDS UP TO 7M (22FT) AWAY.

Local Name: LLS UTX FROM PLATFORM 0 TO PLATFORM 10
Free Text: LIVERPOOL LIME STREET SIGNAL BOX CAR PARK. UTX chambers over 1.2m deep. Full confined space training may be required. REB (signal box car park) to (chamber next to OLE stanchion L193/73A) which goes from 193m890y to 193m871y chambers are 600 x 600 entry points. C1/43 route behind platform 10 wall is from 193m871y to 193m844y then into URX under the RRAP which is from 193m844y to 193m833y. There is then another chamber running from the REB to a chamber within the access road, this run is from 193m890y to 193m912y. The chamber then runs into the UTX chamber H located near to the bin stores 193m912y to 193m966y. All of the chambers and route have Telecomms, Signalling, 650V and 400v power cables.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Limit of 3 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable to all concourse and platform areas. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Senior Asset Manager for consideration.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: The tracks at the station have overhead 25,000-volt AC electric traction supply cables. All works undertaken on the station must be undertaken in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified line. Note: Portable metal steps and ladders <electrical conductors> are specifically banned from Liverpool Lime Street Station in order to avoid the danger of contact with traction current supply equipment.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: There are no 3rd rail electric conductor rails at this station.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: There are no 4th rail electric conductor rails at this station.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: There are numerous power cable runs of both high and low voltage above and below ground level throughout the station. Should there be any possibility whatsoever of cables being disturbed by work activities at a site, it will be necessary for contractors to establish the exact location and nature of cable runs prior to work commencing. This is in order that appropriate measures may be implemented to protect the safety of personnel, and the integrity of the cables and other equipment. In areas which are not currently occupied electrical circuits should be checked before work commences as it cannot be guaranteed that apparently out of service circuits have been isolated.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: In addition to the above cabling, Liverpool Lime Street contains a large quantity of operational equipment including Signalling and Telecommunications <S&T> cables and train dispatch equipment. Any contractor proposing to undertake any demolition, excavation or works that could disturb any cables should refer to the National Hazard Directory. Additional information on S&T equipment can be obtained from the Route Signalling Engineer.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: There are no records of the drainage layout but if they become available they will be held by the Facility Delivery Manager. Contractors should note that drainage exists beneath platforms and in the railway arches below the concourse. No records are held of drainage in the concourse or beneath the station approach, though undoubtedly this will exist to serve buildings in these areas.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: There are no records of water supply pipelines but when they become available they will be held by the Facility Delivery Manager though these will be present in most areas of the station.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Gas is supplied throughout the station. There are gas meters in the Main Switch Room, by the roadway opposite the Red Star Parcels Building and at the bottom of Platform 8. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the Main meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency contact 0800 111 999. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: No records of hot water supply pipelines are held. Central supplied hot water is supplied from the main boiler room located opposite Platform 6 and distributed to the heating system and distributed throughout the station by means of a network of pipes, the majority of which are lagged.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Asbestos hazards please see ARMS for specific details

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: The presence of vermin is controlled by ensuring that rubbish is properly packaged and removed regularly. When infestation is discovered a pest control contractor is brought in to deal with the specific problem. Contractors should be aware that poisoned bait may be positioned at various locations.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: The public and staff toilets within the station have their cisterns etc covered by panels. Operatives should be aware of the possible presence of sharps and biological hazards in these locations.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: This is located to the side of Platform 9. The container is removed for emptying a few times per week. In this area there are electrical, hydraulic and also potentially biological hazards caused by the waste and possible presence of vermin.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: No section of Liverpool Lime Street Station is classed as a sub surface station, although the associated Merseyrail areas are.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Accessed from the common stairway from the concourse as the escalator plant room and the main switch room. It is located down the stairway and left at the second landing. The possibility of various gasses escaping from the disused pipework is a mild cause for concern and because of this, the area should be considered as a confined space and air monitoring carried out before and during work taking place.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: This is accessed by the stairway at the barrier end of the building. The roof contains the air conditioning equipment etc. for the various premises below. The lay out of this equipment can make it difficult to access various areas of the roof. Operatives need to be aware of the electrical hazards and to work within the handrail which runs around the perimeter of the roof.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Roof of retail outlets - off concourse and opposite the Barrier Building. Access to this roof is by ladder through the large hatch in the passageway. There are lines erected to prevent operatives from straying too close to the roof edge. This roof has become a disposal area for used needles/syringes which are disposed of by drug users when leaving the public toilets, extra care is to be taken when working on this roof area.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: The Train Shed Roof has three access stairways: - Behind the Train Crew Building off Platform 1, - On Platform 6, - to the side of Platform 9 and adjacent to the access roadway leading to the Signal Box car park. Walkways, stairs and a series of cradles have been designed and are provided for maintenance purposes. Access is limited to authorised persons only.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: There is believed to be access to the escalator roof is from the yard in the `Head of Steam? public house. Operatives working in this area need to be mindful of how their work practices will impact on this area. Access for authorised persons only.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: There are numerous manholes throughout the station. It is believed that none of these are deep but they should be considered as confined spaces until discovered otherwise. The hazards present will depend on the manhole purpose but will include electrical and biological hazards.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Located opposite Platform 6. The entrance is at the bottom of a stairway, through the double doors to the left. The pipework is marked and lagged reducing the likelihood of contact with hot pipework. This is a very warm room and the high temperature will be hazardous to those working in the area for prolonged periods. Only authorised persons may enter this room.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Located on concourse next to stairway. Accessed by the common stairway for the Escalator Plant Room and the disused Gents Toilets. It is located at the bottom of the stairway and the electrical equipment extends into other areas of the basement beyond this switch room. There is a diagram of the electrical layout of the station on one of the walls. The expected electrical hazards are present and as with other switch rooms only authorised persons may enter. There are often no warning signs on entry doors indicating equipment voltages but access is restricted to authorised personnel only.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Located opposite Platform 6 and adjacent to the Boiler Room, the entrance door is straight ahead from the bottom of the stairway. All the cables are located above head height on trays. The expected electrical hazards are present and as with other switch rooms only authorised persons may enter.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Located opposite Platform 4. The expected electrical hazards are present and as with other switch rooms only authorised persons may enter.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: There are a number of electrical switchboards located throughout the station serving various offices, workshops, etc. These are Customer Services Managers Office, Room 16 and The Main Office, 3A. The expected electrical hazards are present and as with other switch rooms only authorised persons may enter.

ELR Name: EDGE HILL JN - LIVERPOOL LIME STREET(L,POOL - MANCR EXCH LINE)(L18)
Free Text: Located in Travel Centre. There are a number of electrical switchboards located throughout the station serving various offices, workshops, etc. One is in the corridor opposite the mess room. The expected electrical hazards are present and as with other switch rooms only authorised persons may enter.

Free Text: Located in basement beyond the main switch room. There are a number of gas meters within the station premises with the obvious hazard of fire being present. This room should be considered a confined space.

Free Text: Located at the end of platform 8. There are a number of gas meters within the station premises with the obvious hazard of fire being present. At the end of Platform 8, the gas meter is housed in a box.

Operatives working in this area will need to take the appropriate control measures to deal with risk of passing traffic.
